description:
Goal: replace the stale `expanded macro-free AST` assumption with a compiler2-owned source-production layer. Submitted source becomes canonical Fz-shaped quoted AST allocated on a compile-time Fz process/source heap; source expansion, built-in source forms, and user macros all operate on that same value representation and publish the same downstream facts compiler2 already consumes: `ModuleIndexed`, `ModuleDefined`, `FunctionDefined`, `TypeDefined`, protocol dispatch facts, and function-contract/type-reference wait sets.

Grounding:
- Current compiler2 source jobs parse into shared `src/ast::Item` / `src/ast::Expr` trees, reject `Item::MacroCall`, and expect captures/pipes/operators to have been desugared before lowering. That is the wrong boundary for fz2.
- Keeping a Rust-shaped source AST as compiler2's canonical source model would preserve the old-world shape and force macro expansion into repeated encode/decode cycles. Macro expansion is recursive and common, so the loop must not copy between Rust-shaped AST and Fz-shaped values.
- The old frontend macro pass has useful semantics, but the wrong substrate: `exec::Value`, a scratch `CompileTimeEvaluator`, a partial AST codec, and eager whole-program expansion.
- Elixir's durable shape is better because parser, compiler, and macro code all manipulate the same BEAM data. The fz equivalent is Fz-shaped runtime data: tuples/lists/atoms/maps on an Fz heap, with Rust builders/readers/cursors over that data where compiler2 needs structure.
- Compiler2 already has the authority we need: stable module/function/type ids, namespace savepoints, fact ownership, and a process heap/runtime value model.
- `AnyValueRef` is a heap-local source transport handle, not a durable fact identity by itself. Compiler2 facts need a stable source identity/fingerprint derived from the quoted value graph, lexical context, and the explicit metadata policy.

Signal:
- `CodeState` / `ModuleSourceKind` no longer store shared `Rc<Item>` as compiler2's source authority; compiler2 source identity is a rooted Fz-shaped quoted AST value plus stable source identity/revision semantics.
- Macro call/return stays in one representation: quoted args, `__CALLER__`, macro results, recursive expansion input, and compiler-service source forms are all Fz values.
- `fz2` accepts representative macro and source-sugar fixtures (`macro_inc`, `cross_module_macro`, `pipe_headless_case`, lambda/capture sugar, and item-macro test surface) without calling the old frontend macro/desugar path.
- Telemetry shows macro-provider waits, source-root identities, and source definition publication as ordinary compiler2 job effects; downstream semantic/artifact jobs still consume only the existing definition/lowering/dispatch facts.
- Redefining a macro or a macro provider changes the produced definition facts by owned source revisions, and only the dependent root frontier re-closes.
- Rebuilding the same quoted source into different heap addresses does not by itself churn semantic facts; changing the quoted source graph or lexical context does.
- The parser/source/docs describe Fz-shaped quoted source production, not a magic pre-expanded Rust AST input.

Strategy:
- Make Fz-shaped quoted AST the single source representation for compiler2. Rust code may have typed builders/readers/cursors over it, but not a second canonical Rust AST that macro expansion repeatedly converts through.
- Treat `{process heap, root}` as the conceptual source transport unit and `AnyValueRef` as the Fz-visible root argument/result. The active compile-time `Process` supplies the heap/world.
- Derive a stable source identity/fingerprint at compiler2 fact boundaries from the quoted AST graph, source-form tags, literal values, lexical context, and the chosen diagnostic metadata policy. Never use raw heap address or old Rust pointer identity as fact equality.
- Bring parser output in-house under compiler2 by having the parser allocate/build Fz-shaped quoted source values directly. The lexer may remain shared only while it is a neutral byte-tokenizer; if it carries old AST policy, fork that too.
- Model compile-time lexical environment as a `Namespace`-backed `CompileEnv`; `__ENV__`, `__MODULE__`, and `__CALLER__` are Fz-value projections of that env, not separate authorities.
- Define compiler services for source forms (`def`, `defmodule`, `defmacro`, `alias`, `import`, `require`, `@type`, `@spec`, protocol forms) as Rust readers over Fz-shaped source values that mutate env/savepoints and publish compiler2 facts.
- Execute macros only after their provider facts are available; macro calls receive quoted Fz-value args plus caller env, return quoted Fz-value AST, and the returned value is recursively expanded before definition/lowering.
- Preserve hygiene by carrying lexical context in quoted identifiers: macro-authored names resolve in the macro definition namespace; unquoted caller AST keeps the caller namespace.
- Remove compiler2's pre-expanded/macro-free assumptions and the partial old AST-value/evaluator dependency from the fz2 path instead of adding adapters.

Pressure-test results folded into the plan:
- `macro_inc`: local macro definitions must be executable before same-scope macro use; a reserved macro name is not enough. Function definition revisions must include the produced body/source identity or macro changes will not wake lowering.
- `cross_module_macro`: `import Helpers, only: [twice: 1]` cannot blindly bind a function. It must wait on Helpers' defined surface so the imported symbol kind is known, then bind `twice/1` as a macro and execute it with caller env.
- `Helpers.twice`: quoted `double(unquote(x))` must keep the macro definition context on `double` while the unquoted `x` keeps the App caller context. Otherwise the expansion resolves the call in the wrong module.
- item macros such as `test(:name) do ... end` should return ordinary quoted source (`def`/compiler-service AST), not the old narrow `:fn_def` tuple decoder.
- `Fz.Compiler.def` from macro IR receives only an AST root, validates it against the active compile-time process, derives source identity from that root, and publishes the same function source fact literal source would publish.
- Reparse/rebuild pressure: two equal quoted source graphs allocated at different heap addresses must compare equal for semantic fact revision; a macro expansion changing `x + 1` to `x + 2` must compare different even if it targets the same `FunctionId`.

Lace: this mini-epic blocks `fz-rh2.11.5`. When it closes, return to `.11.5` for the original surface-parity closeout, then `.11.6` can extend the fz2 fixture harness.

comments.text: Transport invariant added: the unit of quoted source movement is . The heap is the compile-time/source Fz heap that owns the quoted AST graph; the root is the current AST value handle inside that heap. Parser output, macro args,  projections, macro returns, recursive expansion input, and compiler-service source forms move as . A macro returns a new  relative to the same heap; expansion must not copy into a second AST format or return roots from a different heap.

Correction to prior transport note: the unit of quoted source movement is `{heap, root}`. The heap is the compile-time/source Fz heap that owns the quoted AST graph; the root is the current AST value handle inside that heap. Parser output, macro args, `__CALLER__` projections, macro returns, recursive expansion input, and compiler-service source forms move as `{heap, root}`. A macro returns a new `root` relative to the same heap; expansion must not copy into a second AST format or return roots from a different heap.

Source-root ABI clarification: at the Fz-visible boundary an AST root is an AnyValueRef value (usually a tuple/list/atom/map graph). The heap is not passed as a Fz argument; it is implied by the active compile-time Process that ir_interp is running. Conceptually source transport is still {process heap, root}, but the service/macro ABI passes root values and validates them against the current process heap/world. Fz.Compiler service externs use that same context to save roots as function AST/source facts.

Job-model correction: indexing/scoping/module definition are not the only consumers that must move. CodeState/module source/function source and jobs such as contract derivation, body lowering, dispatch planning, and keying must read compiler2 Fz AST roots (AnyValueRef in the active compile-time process heap), not old Rc<Item>/FnDef authority. Added a dedicated child ticket so compiler services/macros depend on that cut.

Plan correction: source identity is not a heap-independent structural digest. The durable compiler2 source key is {heap, root}; structural fingerprints are layered on top for semantic comparison/revision policy. Downstream tickets should preserve that split when moving parser output, job state, and compiler services onto quoted source roots.

Plan correction after design review: (1)  is not a second  authority. The right shape is one scope snapshot rooted in namespace plus current module/function/source metadata, with , , and the needed  fields projected from that snapshot at macro/source boundaries. (2)  belongs in  as a source pre-scan dependency workflow: wait on  to learn the exported macro surface, then wait on the exact selected macro executable facts ( or all minus ). (3)  should reuse the shared function lowering / IR substrate for macros; the difference is compile-time ABI and readiness, not a second macro-only lowering implementation. Elixir's / and dispatch paths were the reference check for this correction.

title: Compiler2 source production: quoted AST, macro facts, and compiler services
